steps.title: Identify the task
steps.content: The student wants to use a quotation to challenge Thucydides’s explanation that “the rise of Athens” caused the conflict. The correct choice must (1) include a quotation and (2) directly contradict Thucydides’s claim.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Check each choice against both requirements:
  • Choice A — ‘According to Allison’s Thucydides trap theory, whenever “a rising power is threatening to displace a ruling power,” conflict is likely.’: Uses a quotation, but it restates Allison’s theory, which is based on Thucydides’s explanation — it supports rather than challenges Thucydides. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Thucydides wrote that conflict between the two powers was “inevitable,” although Stewart later challenged the historical basis of this claim.’: Mentions a challenge but the only quotation used is from Thucydides himself, not from Stewart’s counter-argument. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘According to Stewart, a “clash of cultures” between Athens and Sparta caused the conflict, not Athens’s rise.’: Quotes Stewart’s counter-explanation and directly contradicts Thucydides’s claim that Athens’s rise caused the conflict. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Thucydides explained that conflict was caused by “the rise of Athens and the fear this instilled in Sparta,” but Allison disagreed, seeing the conflict as an example of the Thucydides trap.’: Misrepresents the notes — Allison’s theory affirms, not challenges, Thucydides’s explanation. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
